name: quant-research
description: Protocol skills for algorithmic trading and quantitative finance research. One notebook = one Purpose (a research thesis: the parent claim about the world the notebook tests, verdicted at notebook closure); each `## H<id>` block is one experiment that tests an admissible child claim decomposing the parent thesis (own falsifiable claim, own stated purpose distinct from parent, meaningful research unit, AND citation of a specific terminal answer from a why-why chain (5-whys, ≥3 levels, mechanism-level termination, every level observation-pinned to a cited table cell / distribution / correlation — prose-only plausibility levels are rejected; when computation is unavailable the chain stops and surfaces the gap rather than completing from inference) of the parent H — the bare verdict / failure_mode label is not a legitimate root for derivation; sweeps go in robustness battery). Schema uses `purpose_id` so `experiment` is reserved for the apparatus that tests one hypothesis. Bundles `quant-research` (research design with up-front headline-figure plan, time-series validation, robustness, multi-agent bug-review layer with adversarial cold-eye reviewer) and `experiment-review` (multi-agent research-quality / claim-warrant review with adversarial cold-eye reviewer; mandatory co-gate with bug-review before verdict='supported').
